name: maxtac-source
description: Static analysis, optional codebase memory, source scan closure, source CVE/patch-history loops, invariant loops, deep source scan loops, thin exact-path closure, negative-evidence inputs, and external finding intake workflows for source code and existing decompiler output in MaxTAC research.

interface.shortDescription: SAST, codebase memory, source loops, scan closure, negative evidence, intake, CFG, and OpenGrep evidence.
interface.longDescription: MaxTAC for Source adds static surface triage, optional codebase-memory-mcp graph usage, source CVE and patch-history correlation loops, invariant-driven source audit loops, deep source scan loops over functions and fields, source diff/repo scan worklists with coverage receipts, thin exact-path source closure for tiny non-reportable targets, negative-evidence inputs for Core false-negative review, external finding intake normalization, focused control-flow evidence, and OpenGrep rule guidance for source code or existing decompiler output. It does not own reverse engineering; pair it with the domain pack that produces the code view, such as Binary or Android.
defaultPrompt: Use MaxTAC for Source to triage this authorized source target.
Use MaxTAC for Source with codebase memory to map this repository before triage.
Use MaxTAC for Source to run a CVE and patch-history loop for this codebase.
Use MaxTAC for Source to run an invariant-driven source audit loop.
Use MaxTAC for Source to run a deep scan loop over every security-relevant function and field.
Create a focused MaxTAC Source packet for this route or subsystem.
Run a MaxTAC Source scan with coverage receipts for this diff.
Feed these Source scan receipts into a Core false-negative review.
Create a thin MaxTAC Source closure for this one-file helper.
Normalize these external security findings for MaxTAC triage.
